name: Pastéis de Nata
description: Portugal's iconic custard tarts with a flaky pastry shell and creamy, slightly scorched custard filling.

recipeIngredient.display: Ground cinnamon and icing sugar, to serve

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 250°C / 230°C fan. Lightly grease a 12-hole muffin tin.
Roll the puff pastry into a tight log. Cut into 12 rounds and press each into the muffin holes, pushing up the sides to form a cup.
In a saucepan, heat milk, cream, cinnamon stick, and lemon zest to just below boiling. Remove from heat.
Whisk sugar, flour, and egg yolks together. Gradually pour in the hot milk, whisking constantly. Remove cinnamon and zest.
Fill the pastry cups to just below the rim. Bake for 12–15 minutes until the pastry is golden and the custard is speckled with dark spots.
Cool slightly then dust with icing sugar and ground cinnamon. Best eaten warm.

notes.title: Authentic touch
notes.text: The dark scorched spots on the custard are traditional and desirable — don't take them out too early.
